pageTitle: Gravity Design System Storybook | Vani Balasundaram

hero.title: Building Gravity Design System Storybook from Scratch
hero.description: How I built a functional Storybook documentation using Cursor AI to bridge the gap between design and engineering — enabling engineers to quickly access production-ready code from our Figma-based design system.

main.heading: Bridging Design & Engineering
paragraphs: At Doctor Anywhere, we had a comprehensive design system called Gravity built in Figma. While Figma served as our source of truth for design, engineers often struggled to translate designs into code efficiently.

The gap between design specifications and production code was causing delays, inconsistencies, and frustration across teams. Engineers needed a functional documentation where they could directly access and copy code snippets.
subparagraphs: Build a Storybook documentation from scratch that would serve as a living, functional reference for engineers — complementing Figma as the design source of truth while providing production-ready code for implementation.

insights.title: Figma Limitations
insights.body: Figma is great for design but engineers need actual code, not just visual specs.

insights.title: Inconsistent Implementation
insights.body: Without code reference, engineers interpreted designs differently across platforms.

insights.title: Time Consuming
insights.body: Engineers spent too much time figuring out how to implement components from scratch.

insights.title: White Label Needs
insights.body: Our white label portal needed consistent, reusable components across different brands.

intro.heading: Trying Multiple Methods
intro.body: Building a Storybook from scratch wasn't straightforward. I explored multiple approaches to find the most efficient and cost-effective solution that would work for our team.

features.title: Using Cursor AI as My Co-Pilot
features.body: I leveraged Cursor AI to accelerate the development process. As a designer building something technical, Cursor helped me write functional code, debug issues, and implement best practices — all while learning along the way.

features.title: Figma as Source of Truth
features.body: While Storybook became the code reference, Figma remained our design source of truth. I ensured that every component in Storybook matched its Figma counterpart exactly — creating a seamless bridge between design and development.

features.title: Cross-Team Collaboration
features.body: Working with cross-functional teams helped us find cost-saving methods for launching and hosting the Storybook. We ultimately deployed it on Bitbucket's static hosting, which was both free and integrated with our existing workflow.
list: Engineering team input on code structure

visuals.alt: Storybook component tree with Forms and Foundation

intro.heading: A Functional Storybook for Engineers
intro.body: The final Storybook is a comprehensive, functional documentation that engineers can use to quickly understand and implement Gravity design system components. It's not just documentation — it's a working code reference.

visuals.alt: Storybook component tree with Forms and Foundation

cards.title: 📦 Component Library
cards.body: All Gravity design system components documented with interactive examples and copy-ready code snippets.

cards.title: 🎨 Design Tokens
cards.body: Colors, typography, spacing, and other tokens documented and ready for implementation.

cards.title: 📱 Responsive Preview
cards.body: Components can be previewed at different viewport sizes to ensure responsive implementation.

cards.title: 🔧 Props Documentation
cards.body: Clear documentation of component props, variants, and usage guidelines for engineers.

results.label: White Label Support
results.body: Enables consistent component implementation across white label portal variants

results.label: Cross-Platform
results.body: Components documented for use across web, mobile, and other platforms

results.label: Hosting Cost
results.body: Deployed on Bitbucket static hosting — completely free infrastructure

testimonial.quote: Building this Storybook taught me that designers can bridge the gap between design and engineering. With the right tools like Cursor AI, we can create functional solutions that truly help our engineering partners ship faster and more consistently.

cards.title: Designers Can Build
cards.body: With AI tools like Cursor, designers can create functional technical solutions without being full-stack developers. It's about problem-solving, not just coding.

cards.title: Cross-Team Value
cards.body: Collaborating with engineering and DevOps early helped find cost-effective solutions and ensured the final product actually met their needs.

cards.title: Documentation is Product
cards.body: Good documentation is a product in itself. It needs UX thinking, iteration, and user feedback — just like any other product we design.

cards.title: Start Scrappy, Iterate
cards.body: Trying multiple methods before finding the right approach is part of the process. The key is to start, learn, and iterate quickly.

blocks.title: See It Live
blocks.body: The Gravity Design System Storybook is live and being used by engineers at Doctor Anywhere. Check it out to see how design systems can be documented for maximum engineering efficiency.
